name: Tulsi-Ginger Kadha
tagline: Holy basil, ginger, black pepper, and clove brew — anti-inflammatory immune drink
highlight: Boil and steep — short boils preserve the volatile antioxidants in tulsi

hero.alt: Steaming amber-green herbal brew in a glass cup with tulsi leaves and ginger
hero.caption: tulsi-ginger kadha — boiled, strained, sipped

healthFacts: Tulsi (holy basil) has eugenol and rosmarinic acid — anti-inflammatory adaptogens.
Ginger reduces hs-CRP and supports cardiovascular and digestive health.
Black pepper's piperine boosts absorption of co-consumed phytochemicals.
Sugar-free version is appropriate for daily consumption — sweeten only when ill.

steps.title: Bring to a boil
steps.detail: Add water, ginger, peppercorns, cloves, cinnamon, and cardamom to a small pot. Bring to a vigorous boil.
steps.tip: Crushing the spices before they go in releases their oils faster.

steps.title: Add tulsi and steep
steps.detail: Add tulsi leaves. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes. The liquid should darken to a tea-amber green.
steps.tip: Boiling tulsi too long destroys the volatile oils — 5 minutes max.

steps.title: Strain and sip
steps.detail: Strain into a cup. Stir in honey/jaggery and a few drops of lemon if using. Sip slow while still hot.
steps.tip: Add honey only after the kadha has cooled below 60°C — heat destroys honey's enzymes.
